title: Staples launches Klarna flexible payments, helping consumers to ‘work now, pay later’
full_text:
Klarna continues growth streak with over 100,000 merchants added this year 

New York, 15 November, 2024 – Klarna, the AI-powered global payments network and shopping assistant, has launched with Staples, a leader in workspace products and solutions. 

Staples customers can now spread the cost of their vital office supplies with Klarna’s Pay in 4 or Financing options, or pay in full with Pay Now, when shopping online at Staples.com.

“We’re excited to provide this added convenience and value to our customers, making their shopping experience more seamless than ever,” said Mike Shady, Chief Digital Officer, Staples, Inc. “This partnership with Klarna reflects our commitment to evolving with our customers’ needs by making their shopping experience even more accessible and allowing them to easily manage purchases without the pressure of upfront costs.”

"We’re thrilled to team up with Staples to make it easier for shoppers to get what they need for work and home. Our goal is for Klarna to be available on every checkout, for everything, everywhere,” said Erin Jaeger, Head of North America at Klarna. “Our users’ top Klarna wishlist items are electronic devices, including laptops, monitors, and headphones; by joining forces with Staples, we’re able to offer our customers an easier way to spread the cost of vital office supplies fairly and transparently.”

With Klarna’s flexible payment options, Staples customers now have more ways to pay. Whether stocking up on home office essentials, grabbing a new laptop, or upgrading headphones, shoppers can choose to pay now or split their purchase into four interest-free payments, or for bigger buys, extend payments even further. These options bring a simple, budget-friendly way to shop for everything they need to stay productive. 

This new partnership expands Klarna’s rapidly growing network of partners, which it has grown by over 100,000 over the past year. Klarna now works with 46 of the top 100 US retailers, including trusted names such as Airbnb, Uber, Adidas, Expedia, Rite Aid and many, many others.
